ucs.title: [LLM] TG-3390 / Emissary Panda DLL side-loading via signed third-party binary dropping HyperBro/PlugX
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature side-loading chain — a legitimate, normally-signed binary (e.g. Symantec, Trend Micro, Notepad++ gup.exe, Microsoft Office helper) executed from an unusual user-writable folder where it loads an attacker-supplied DLL co-located in the same directory. TG-3390 (BRONZE UNION / APT27 / LuckyMouse) has used this pattern for years to load HyperBro, SysUpdate and PlugX while evading AV vendor signatures.
ucs.splunk:
`sysmon` EventCode=7 (Image="*\\rc.exe" OR Image="*\\oleview.exe" OR Image="*\\SymantecHelpUI.exe" OR Image="*\\OfficeClickToRun.exe" OR Image="*\\wsc_proxy.exe" OR Image="*\\gup.exe" OR Image="*\\TosBtKbd.exe" OR Image="*\\PotPlayerMini.exe" OR Image="*\\vmtoolsd.exe") (ImageLoaded="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR ImageLoaded="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR ImageLoaded="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR ImageLoaded="*\\PerfLogs\\*") NOT (ImageLoaded="*\\Windows\\*" OR ImageLoaded="*\\Program Files*\\*")
| eval host_dir=replace(Image, "\\\\[^\\\\]+$", ""), dll_dir=replace(ImageLoaded, "\\\\[^\\\\]+$", "")
| where host_dir==dll_dir
| stats count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Hashes) as hashes by host User Image ImageLoaded
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let _sideload_hosts = dynamic(["rc.exe","oleview.exe","SymantecHelpUI.exe","mscorsvw.exe","OfficeClickToRun.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","gup.exe","TosBtKbd.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","hkcmd.exe"]);
let _writable_paths = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Users\Default\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_sideload_hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_writable_paths)            // host bin from non-canonical install path = the TG-3390 tell
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath                        // DLL co-located with the loader (search-order hijack)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
   and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | project DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId = ProcessId, ProcessCreationTime, SpawnCmd = ProcessCommandLine, SpawnParent = InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          User = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          HostBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          HostPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          HostSha256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          LoadedDll = FileName,
          DllPath = FolderPath,
          DllSha256 = SHA256,
          SpawnParent, SpawnCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _sideload_hosts = dynamic([@"\rc.exe", @"\oleview.exe", @"\SymantecHelpUI.exe", @"\OfficeClickToRun.exe", @"\wsc_proxy.exe", @"\gup.exe", @"\TosBtKbd.exe", @"\PotPlayerMini.exe", @"\vmtoolsd.exe", @"\hkcmd.exe"]);
let _writable_paths = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7              // Sysmon ImageLoad
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Hashes      = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User)
| where Image has_any (_sideload_hosts)
| where Image has_any (_writable_paths)                                       // loader running from suspicious dir
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| extend HostDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"),
         DllDir  = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")
| where HostDir =~ DllDir                                                     // co-located DLL
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\") and not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Signed, Hashes
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TG-3390 / Emissary Panda DLL Sideload via Signed Third-Party Loader
id: 7e3b9a40-9f0e-4d6f-9e93-3a7c10c6b9aa
status: experimental
description: Detects DLL side-loading pattern characteristic of Threat Group-3390 (APT27 / BRONZE UNION / LuckyMouse) where a legitimate signed binary is dropped into a user-writable directory along with a malicious co-located DLL (HyperBro / SysUpdate / PlugX loaders).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0027/
  - https://www.secureworks.com/research/bronze-union
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.001
  - g0027
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection_loader:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rc.exe'
      - '\oleview.exe'
      - '\SymantecHelpUI.exe'
      - '\OfficeClickToRun.exe'
      - '\wsc_proxy.exe'
      - '\gup.exe'
      - '\TosBtKbd.exe'
      - '\PotPlayerMini.exe'
      - '\vmtoolsd.exe'
      - '\hkcmd.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
  filter_legit:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Portable installations of legitimate apps run from user-writable paths (rare in managed estates)
level: high
ucs.rationale: TG-3390 has been observed for over a decade abusing legitimate signed third-party executables (Symantec helpers, Notepad++ gup.exe, Office Click-to-Run, VMware Tools) as DLL side-load hosts for HyperBro, SysUpdate and PlugX. The actor's tradecraft tell is dropping the loader+DLL pair into user-writable directories (\Users\Public\, \ProgramData\, AppData\Temp), which the canonical install paths exc
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] TG-3390 China Chopper / ASPXSpy webshell — IIS/Exchange w3wp.exe spawning recon & credential-access binaries
ucs.description: Hunts post-exploitation activity from China Chopper (caidao) and ASPXSpy webshells that TG-3390 plants on internet-facing IIS, OWA and SharePoint servers as a foothold and long-term persistence channel. The detection fires when w3wp.exe (or the Exchange equivalent) spawns interactive shells or recon/credential-theft tooling — a transition that should never occur during normal IIS request handling.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_id) as pids
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe"
      AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","mshta.exe","rar.exe")
    by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.dest
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval risk_score=case(match(process_name,"ntdsutil|rundll32|rar|certutil"),90, match(process_name,"powershell|cmd|wmic|reg"),70, true(),50)
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - risk_score lastTime
ucs.kql:
let _webshell_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","mshta.exe","rar.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ (_webshell_children)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                            // suppress machine-account housekeeping
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
| extend HighRisk = iff(FileName in~ ("ntdsutil.exe","rar.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","vssadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe"), "yes","no")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AppPool, AccountName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSha256 = SHA256,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemote = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          HighRisk
| order by HighRisk desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _webshell_children = dynamic([@"\cmd.exe", @"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe", @"\cscript.exe", @"\wscript.exe", @"\net.exe", @"\net1.exe", @"\whoami.exe", @"\systeminfo.exe", @"\ipconfig.exe", @"\tasklist.exe", @"\reg.exe", @"\sc.exe", @"\wmic.exe", @"\nltest.exe", @"\quser.exe", @"\ntdsutil.exe", @"\vssadmin.exe", @"\rundll32.exe", @"\certutil.exe", @"\bitsadmin.exe", @"\hh.exe", @"\mshta.exe", @"\rar.exe", @"\procdump.exe", @"\procdump64.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                                       // process-create with cmdline
| where ParentProcessName endswith @"\w3wp.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any (_webshell_children)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)", 1, tostring(EventData))
| extend HighRisk = iff(NewProcessName has_any (@"\ntdsutil.exe", @"\rar.exe", @"\procdump.exe", @"\vssadmin.exe", @"\rundll32.exe", @"\certutil.exe"), "yes", "no")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, AppPool, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType, HighRisk
| order by HighRisk desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TG-3390 China Chopper / ASPXSpy Webshell Post-Exploitation
id: c5d2e1b4-2f7a-4cd9-9b91-1f0a4ec23bb1
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned by w3wp.exe characteristic of Threat Group-3390 (APT27 / Emissary Panda) post-webshell activity (China Chopper, ASPXSpy).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0027/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S0020/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S0073/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1059.003
  - g0027
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\ntdsutil.exe'
      - '\vssadmin.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IIS-hosted automation that intentionally shells out (rare; should be allowlisted by AppPool)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: TG-3390/Emissary Panda is one of the most prolific operators of China Chopper and ASPXSpy webshells on Exchange/OWA, SharePoint, and IIS targets across aerospace, defense and government — Secureworks' BRONZE UNION reporting documents this as their go-to long-term persistence model. Once the actor authenticates to the webshell, IIS w3wp.exe spawns interactive shells and recon/credential tools (whoa
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.
